title: Clean up test_sse.py: redundant imports, fixture naming, teardown
analysis:
This is a post-review cleanup task for tests/test_sse.py after task 89 consolidated _parse_sse_body. Four changes needed, all confined to a single file:

1. REDUNDANT IMPORTS: TestParseSSEBodyFromConftest (lines 327, 337, 346) has per-method `from tests.conftest import _parse_sse_body` imports that shadow the module-level import on line 15. These are dead code — removing them is safe since all other test methods in the file already use the module-level import successfully.

2. FIXTURE RENAME: `_sse_db_path` (line 19) uses a leading underscore, inconsistent with `e2e_db_path` in test_web_e2e.py and standard pytest conventions (leading underscore implies private/non-fixture). Referenced in 4 places within test_sse.py: fixture def name (line 19), sse_db parameter (line 25), sse_db body (line 27), and sse_app parameter (line 33).

3. TEARDOWN: `sse_db` fixture (lines 24-29) uses bare `return db` with no teardown. The project convention (see `e2e_db` in test_web_e2e.py lines 40-47 and `catalog_db` in conftest.py lines 76-89) is try/yield/finally with db.close(). CatalogDB.close() calls self.conn.close().

4. DOCSTRING: `sse_app` fixture takes `sse_db` as a parameter but never uses it — it's injected purely for ordering so the DB schema exists before create_app runs, not for direct use. Unlike `e2e_app` which only takes `e2e_db_path`, `sse_app` needs this ordering hint because create_app opens its own connection. The docstring should explain this.

prerequisites.description: Verify all existing test_sse.py tests pass before making changes (baseline confirmation)

steps.description: Write a test in TestSSEFixtureTeardown that verifies the sse_db fixture properly closes its DB connection after teardown. The test should capture a reference to the db, then after the fixture scope ends, check that operating on the connection raises an error. This test should FAIL with the current bare-return fixture since no close() is called.

Convert sse_db fixture from `return db` to `try: yield db / finally: db.close()`. Add `Generator` return type hint (from typing import Generator). This matches the e2e_db pattern in test_web_e2e.py lines 40-47. The teardown test from step-1 should now pass.

Run pytest tests/test_sse.py::TestParseSSEBodyFromConftest -v to confirm the 3 existing tests pass as baseline before removing redundant imports. These tests currently use per-method imports but the module-level import on line 15 will take over.

Remove the 3 redundant `from tests.conftest import _parse_sse_body` imports from TestParseSSEBodyFromConftest methods (lines 327, 337, 346). The module-level import on line 15 already provides the name in module scope.
Run full test_sse.py to confirm all fixture-dependent tests pass before the rename. This is the green baseline for the _sse_db_path -> sse_db_path rename.

Rename _sse_db_path fixture to sse_db_path (remove leading underscore). Update all 4 references: (1) fixture def name on line 19, (2) sse_db fixture parameter on line 25, (3) CatalogDB() call on line 27, (4) sse_app fixture parameter on line 33. Matches e2e_db_path naming convention.

Update sse_app fixture docstring to explain that the sse_db parameter is injected for fixture ordering — it ensures CatalogDB creates the schema before create_app opens its own connection to the same DB file. The parameter is not used directly in the fixture body.
Run full test_sse.py suite to confirm all changes work together: teardown test passes, import cleanup didn't break anything, fixture rename is consistent, and all 13+ tests are green.

design_decisions.decision: Use db.close() in teardown rather than db.conn.close()
design_decisions.rationale: The task description mentions db.conn.close() but the project convention in conftest.py catalog_db fixture (line 89) and e2e_db in test_web_e2e.py (line 47) both use db.close(). CatalogDB.close() delegates to self.conn.close() internally. Using the public API is more robust and consistent.

design_decisions.decision: Add Generator return type hint to sse_db fixture
design_decisions.rationale: Converting from return to yield changes the fixture into a generator. Both e2e_db (test_web_e2e.py line 40) and catalog_db (conftest.py line 76) use the Generator type hint for yield-based fixtures. Consistency requires adding it here too.

design_decisions.decision: Keep sse_db parameter on sse_app fixture and document it rather than removing it
design_decisions.rationale: The parameter serves as an implicit ordering dependency — pytest evaluates sse_db (creating the DB schema) before sse_app (calling create_app which opens its own connection). Removing it would break test setup. A docstring explaining the ordering intent is the correct fix.

design_decisions.decision: Order changes as: teardown first, then imports, then rename, then docstring
design_decisions.rationale: Teardown is the only change with a meaningful new test (TDD). Import cleanup and rename are mechanical refactors validated by existing tests. Docstring is pure documentation. This ordering minimizes merge conflicts between steps.

reuse.how: Copy the exact try/yield/finally structure for sse_db, replacing e2e_db_path with sse_db_path

reuse.what: catalog_db fixture pattern (Generator type hint, yield, close)
reuse.how: Reference for the Generator type hint import and fixture typing convention
The target naming pattern for the _sse_db_path rename — public fixture names should not have leading underscores
Already exists and provides _parse_sse_body to the entire module scope — the redundant per-method imports in TestParseSSEBodyFromConftest are unnecessary
